title: AI Opportunities by Business Function
summary: Maps Joz business-value opportunities by function across finance, ERP, HR, marketing, sales, and leadership rather than abstract AI categories.
body:
The strongest way to extend Joz for business value is by mapping AI opportunities by business function rather than by generic AI category.

Core function areas:

1. Finance and accounting
- invoice handling
- AP and AR workflows
- close support
- spend visibility
- forecasting support
- anomaly detection

2. ERP and operations
- procurement
- exception handling
- inventory visibility
- planning support
- supplier coordination
- fulfillment bottleneck analysis

3. HR and workforce
- recruiting support
- onboarding
- policy Q&A
- manager support
- internal service workflows
- training and knowledge reuse

4. Marketing
- campaign planning
- reporting synthesis
- segmentation
- content operations
- attribution interpretation
- CAC efficiency support

5. Sales and revenue operations
- lead qualification
- proposal and follow-up support
- CRM hygiene
- forecasting assistance
- churn and renewal signals
- next-best action

6. Leadership and transformation
- cross-functional reporting
- operating model redesign
- KPI synthesis
- scenario support
- AI adoption governance

This is useful because leaders usually buy outcomes by function, not abstract agent categories.

claims: The strongest business-value framing is to map AI opportunities by function rather than by generic AI category.
Finance, ERP, HR, marketing, sales, and leadership each have distinct AI opportunity patterns.
Function-specific framing makes Joz commercially clearer to decision-makers.
proof_points: Joz already has financial-services, enterprise-transformation, and decision-intelligence proof across multiple regulated and scaled environments.
The current knowledge base already supports workflow redesign and business-value positioning.
Department-level framing is a natural extension of the existing business-need lane.
